steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: adverbial phrase (“Upon recovering two years later,”).
After the blank: participial phrase (“forcing an angered Richard from the royal court and precipitating a series of battles...”).
The blank must contain an independent clause whose subject will be modified by the following participial phrase.

steps.title: Apply the logical modification rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase modifies the subject of the main clause, and that modification must be logically appropriate. In “Henry resumed his reign, forcing an angered Richard from the royal court,” Henry is the subject, and it logically makes sense that Henry (a person) would force Richard from court.

Choices B and C make “the reign” the grammatical subject, which would illogically suggest the reign itself (not Henry) forced Richard out. Choice D unnecessarily complicates the sentence with a cleft construction.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
